arguments.name: msbuild-review
arguments.prompt:
Review all MSBuild project files in this .NET solution for anti-patterns, duplication, modernization opportunities, and best practices violations. The solution is at /tmp/sv-ec7962dbc4b84b738a59223acdabe0da/ContosoApp.sln with source in src/ and tests in test/.

Specifically look for:
1. Duplicated properties across .csproj files that should be centralized in Directory.Build.props
2. Scattered package versions that should use Central Package Management
3. Anti-patterns (hardcoded paths, restated SDK defaults, Exec instead of built-in tasks, etc.)
4. Modernization opportunities (legacy patterns, unnecessary explicit includes, etc.)
5. Missing Directory.Build.props/targets that could simplify the projects
6. Any other MSBuild best practices violations

Produce a comprehensive, actionable report with specific recommendations and examples of what to change.

data.content:
I now have a complete picture of all 5 project files and the solution structure. Here is my comprehensive review.

---

# MSBuild Code Review Report — ContosoApp Solution

## Solution Overview

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| Solution | `ContosoApp.sln` — 5 projects (3 src, 2 test) |
| SDK Style | ✅ All projects use SDK-style format |
| TFM | `net8.0` across all projects |
| Directory.Build.props | ❌ **Missing** |
| Directory.Build.targets | ❌ **Missing** |
| Directory.Packages.props | ❌ **Missing** (no Central Package Management) |

---

## 🔴 Errors — Likely to Cause Issues

### E1: Package Version Skew — `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`

The same package is referenced at **two different versions** across the solution:

| Project | Version |
|---------|---------|
| `ContosoApp.Core` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Api` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | **8.0.1** |

Since `ContosoApp.Api` references both `Core` (8.0.0) and `Services` (8.0.1), NuGet will resolve this at build time — but the mismatch is a maintenance hazard and can cause subtle runtime issues. At minimum it triggers `NU1605` warnings in strict resolve mode.

### E2: Test Framework Version Skew

The two test projects use **different versions** of the same test infrastructure:

| Package | `Core.Tests` | `Api.Tests` |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |

This means your two test projects run on different test runner versions, which can produce inconsistent behavior, different diagnostics, and mask test failures that only reproduce with one version.

---

## 🟡 Warnings — Anti-patterns That Should Be Fixed

### W1: Massive Property Duplication Across All 5 Projects (→ create `Directory.Build.props`)

The following **7 properties** are copy-pasted verbatim into every single `.csproj`:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

That's **35 duplicated lines** (7 × 5 projects). When you upgrade the TFM to `net9.0`, you'll need to edit 5 files instead of 1.

**Fix:** Create a root-level `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

### W2: Duplicated `DocumentationFile` in All 3 Source Projects (→ centralize)

All three source projects contain:
```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```

**Fix:** Add to `Directory.Build.props` with a condition that excludes test projects:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

### W3: Duplicated `PrintBuildInfo` Target in All 3 Source Projects (→ create `Directory.Build.targets`)

All three source projects have this identical target:
```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

**Fix:** Create a root-level `Directory.Build.targets`:

```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
          Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

### W4: Scattered Package Versions — No Central Package Management

There are **7 distinct packages** referenced across 5 projects, with versions hardcoded inline at each usage site. This is the root cause of errors E1 and E2.

**Fix:** Create `Directory.Packages.props` at the repo root:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Runtime -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <!-- Testing -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then remove all `Version="..."` attributes from `<PackageReference>` in every `.csproj`.

### W5: Test Projects Duplicate `IsPackable=false` (→ centralize)

Both test projects set `<IsPackable>false</IsPackable>`. This should be centralized.

**Fix:** Add to `Directory.Build.props`:
```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
</PropertyGroup>
```

### W6: Missing `PrivateAssets="all"` on `xunit.runner.visualstudio`

In both test projects:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
```

This test runner adapter is a build/development-only tool and should **never** flow as a transitive dependency to consuming projects. Without `PrivateAssets="all"`, it leaks into the dependency graph.

**Fix:**
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" PrivateAssets="all" />
```

### W7: Multiple `<PropertyGroup>` Blocks That Could Be Consolidated

All 3 source projects split properties into 2 separate `<PropertyGroup>` sections (the main one at the top, and a standalone `<DocumentationFile>` block). These should be a single block for clarity — though once `DocumentationFile` moves to `Directory.Build.props` (W2), this becomes moot.

---

## 🔵 Suggestions — Improvements for Maintainability

### S1: Consolidate Common Test Dependencies via `Directory.Build.props`

Both test projects reference the same 4 packages. After adopting CPM (W4), further reduce duplication by adding shared test references to `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<!-- In Directory.Build.props -->
<ItemGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
  <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
  <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" PrivateAssets="all" />
  <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This means test `.csproj` files only need their `<ProjectReference>` — zero boilerplate.

### S2: Consider Replacing `Newtonsoft.Json` with `System.Text.Json`

`ContosoApp.Services` references `Newtonsoft.Json 13.0.3`, while `ContosoApp.Core` and `ContosoApp.Api` use `System.Text.Json 8.0.5`. Having two JSON serializers in the same solution increases attack surface and binary size. Unless you have a specific need for Newtonsoft features (e.g., `JObject` dynamic parsing, complex polymorphism), consider standardizing on `System.Text.Json`.

### S3: The `PrintBuildInfo` Target Has No `Inputs`/`Outputs`

The `PrintBuildInfo` target runs on every build even when nothing has changed. While it's only a `<Message>` task (low cost), adding `Inputs`/`Outputs` or a `Condition` would be the correct incremental build pattern. Alternatively, if this is just for diagnostics, consider removing it from checked-in code.

### S4: Add an `.editorconfig` for Build-Time Code Style Enforcement

All projects set `<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>`, but there's no `.editorconfig` in the repository to define what rules to enforce. Without it, only the default rules apply, which may not match team expectations.

---

## 🟢 Positive — Things Done Well

- ✅ **All SDK-style projects** — no legacy format, no `packages.config`
- ✅ **Nullable reference types enabled** globally
- ✅ **`TreatWarningsAsErrors` enabled** globally — catches issues early
- ✅ **`ImplicitUsings` enabled** — reduces file noise
- ✅ **No `AssemblyInfo.cs` files** — using SDK auto-generation correctly
- ✅ **`EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` enabled** — good for CI consistency
- ✅ **Test projects marked `IsPackable=false`** — correct intent

---

## Summary of Recommended Actions (Priority Order)

| # | Severity | Action | Effort |
|---|----------|--------|--------|
| 1 | 🔴 | Fix version skew by adopting Central Package Management (fixes E1, E2, W4) | Medium |
| 2 | 🟡 | Create `Directory.Build.props` to centralize 7 duplicated properties (W1, W2, W5) | Low |
| 3 | 🟡 | Create `Directory.Build.targets` to centralize the `PrintBuildInfo` target (W3) | Low |
| 4 | 🟡 | Add `PrivateAssets="all"` to `xunit.runner.visualstudio` (W6) | Trivial |
| 5 | 🔵 | Consolidate common test package references in `Directory.Build.props` (S1) | Low |
| 6 | 🔵 | Add `.editorconfig` to complement `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` (S4) | Low |
| 7 | 🔵 | Evaluate replacing `Newtonsoft.Json` with `System.Text.Json` (S2) | Medium |

---

## What the Cleaned-Up Projects Would Look Like

After applying all recommendations, here's the dramatic simplification:

**`src/ContosoApp.Core/ContosoApp.Core.csproj`** (was 26 lines → 9 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**`test/ContosoApp.Core.Tests/ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj`** (was 25 lines → 7 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

That's a **65–72% reduction** in per-project boilerplate, with all policy, versioning, and shared infrastructure managed centrally.
